entries.context: Common mild insult used when someone makes a silly mistake. Used across Spain and Latin America.
entries.suggested_response: Say 'Lo siento' (I'm sorry) and move on.

entries.context: Insulting someone's intelligence. Very common in arguments or road rage.
Very aggressive insult. In some regions can mean 'cuckold'. Signals extreme anger.
entries.suggested_response: Do not respond. Leave calmly — this is a high-tension situation.

entries.context: Common in Mexico and Latin America. Varies in severity by country — always rude.
Used to call someone dirty, greedy, or morally corrupt.
entries.suggested_response: Respond calmly with 'No entiendo' (I don't understand).

entries.context: Accusing someone of stealing. Can draw crowd attention in public places.
entries.suggested_response: Show your belongings calmly. Say 'No soy un ladrón' (I am not a thief).

entries.context: Used as a curse word in anger. Often paired with other insults.
entries.suggested_response: Stay composed. De-escalate by speaking softly.
